summary:
Claim: A high-endemic area for meningococcal meningitis is defined by an incidence exceeding 10 cases per 100,000 population.  
Evidence: - The World Health Organization officially sets >10 cases/100,000 as the threshold for high-burden areas. - Long-term surveillance in sub-Saharan Africa confirms consistent rates above this level.  
Answer: C
